concept: Wipe / Stroke Clean / Clear a Surface
description: To wipe: the table after the meal, the tears from a face, the mist from the window. A light stroke that clears. Finer than 'sawa' (wash — water's work) and gentler than 'rohi' (rub — friction's work).
sound_symbolism: Light 'he' breath, 'we' the stroke across — the sound barely touches the surface, as the act barely does.
grammatical_notes: Transitive: 'mia tomi hewe.' (I wipe the pot.) Of comfort: 'phao lopia menoa hewe.' (The parent wipes the child's face.) Contrast 'sawa' (wash, water's work) and 'rohi' (rub, friction's work).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Wiping the surface is the humble sibling of polishing the mirror — the old metaphor for practice itself, one pass at a time.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The wiped bowl, the wiped blade of the scythe, the wiped brow: maintenance in its smallest, most frequent form.
